steps.title: Avoid a comma splice
steps.content: The first clause before the comma already has a finite verb. The words after the comma should describe the nanoparticles without creating a second independent clause joined only by a comma.

steps.title: Use a participial phrase
steps.content: Choice A, Choice B, and Choice D are finite verb phrases, so each would create a comma splice after the first clause. A present participle forms a modifying phrase instead of a new independent clause. The answer is C.
